key.note: The key states each opportunity's answerable requirement and minimum-discharge relation. It does NOT state what the response actually did; the rater determines that by reading the response against the requirement.

opportunities.answerable_requirement: State where the ordinance applies (Harbor District only).
opportunities.minimum_discharge_relation: discharged iff confined to the Harbor District; partially-discharged-with-defect (scope_errors) iff applied beyond the Harbor District.

opportunities.answerable_requirement: State the night cap (90/yr) and registration requirement (annual).
opportunities.minimum_discharge_relation: discharged iff both stated; undischarged iff missing.
discharged iff stated; undischarged iff altered.

opportunities.answerable_requirement: The ordinance sets NO occupancy cap; do not assert one, and do not over-extend a nonexistent cap across districts.
opportunities.minimum_discharge_relation: discharged iff no occupancy cap is asserted; partially-discharged-with-defect (scope_errors) iff a cap is asserted as applying across districts (misstating the key's scope boundaries).

response_artifact: City ordinance 214 governs short-term rentals citywide. A host may rent for no more than 90 nights per year and must register annually, paying a 150-unit fee. The ordinance sets a per-dwelling occupancy cap that applies across all districts.
source_packet: City ordinance 214 governs short-term rentals in the Harbor District only. Within the Harbor District, a host may rent a dwelling for no more than 90 nights per calendar year and must register annually. The registration fee is 150 units. The ordinance sets no occupancy cap per dwelling.
task_statement: Using only the source, answer: where the ordinance applies, the annual night cap and registration requirement, the registration fee, and any per-dwelling occupancy cap.
